note:
6600万年前，一颗巨大的小行星以大约10亿枚核弹的爆炸能量撞击地球后，一层火山灰、尘埃和蒸发的岩石覆盖了天空，慢慢地落到了地球上。随着动植物物种的集体死亡，被称为有孔虫的微小海底变形虫继续繁殖，用钙和其他深海矿物建造坚固的贝壳，就像它们数亿年来所做的那样。当每个有孔虫不可避免地死亡时--被粉碎成海底沉积物--它们在它们的化石贝壳中保留了地球古代历史的一小部分。
几十年来，科学家们一直在研究这些贝壳，寻找有关古代地球海洋温度、碳收支以及溢出到空气和海洋中的矿物成分的线索。现在，在今天(9月1日)发表的一项新研究中，10)在“科学”杂志上，研究人员分析了数千个有孔虫样本中的化学元素，以建立地球有史以来最详细的气候记录-它揭示了我们目前的气候状况是多么可怕。
这篇新论文包含了几十年的深海钻探任务，形成了一个单一的记录，详细描述了地球在整个新生代的气候波动-6600万年的时间段，从恐龙的死亡开始，一直延伸到现在人类导致气候变化的时代。结果显示，地球是如何通过四个不同的气候状态--被称为沃姆豪斯、霍豪斯、库尔豪斯和冰屋的州--来应对地球轨道、温室气体水平和极地冰盖范围的变化的，这四个州被称为“沃姆豪斯”、“霍豪斯”、“库尔豪斯”和“冰屋”。
曲线图(如上图所示)以一个发人深省的峰值结束。根据研究人员的说法，目前人为造成的全球变暖速度远远超过了新生代任何其他时候看到的自然气候波动，并有可能将我们的星球从漫长的冰屋阶段超级驱动到灼热的温室状态。
加州大学圣克鲁斯分校(University of California，Santa Cruz)地球与行星科学教授詹姆斯·扎霍斯(James Zachos)在一份声明中说，既然我们已经成功捕捉到了自然气候的变异性，我们可以看到，预计的人为变暖将远远超过这一数字。该研究的合著者詹姆斯·扎霍斯(James Zachos)是加州大学圣克鲁斯分校(University of California，Santa Cruz)地球和行星科学教授。政府间气候变化专门委员会(IPCC)预测，在一切照旧的情况下，2300年的全球气温可能会达到5000万年来未曾见过的水平。(IPCC是一个联合国组织，负责评估气候变化对地球的科学、风险和影响。)。
为了编制他们的新纪元气候图，研究作者研究了深海沉积物岩心(岩石、沉积物和微生物的长管)中的有孔虫化石，这些岩芯是在过去几十年里从世界海洋中钻探出来的。有孔虫(有孔虫的缩写)是一种微小的浮游生物，其最古老的近亲大约在10亿年前出现在海洋中；科学家们在海底挖掘得越深，他们发现的有孔虫样本就越古老。
有孔虫贝壳中碳和氧同位素(元素的不同版本)的比率掌握着关键的气候信息。例如，氧同位素氧-18和氧-16之间的比率可以揭示有孔虫建造贝壳时周围的水有多热；比率越高，水就越冷。碳-13和碳-12之间的比率显示了微生物可以食用的有机碳的数量；在这里，比率越高，大气中的温室气体(如二氧化碳)越多。
由于该团队的气候记录涵盖了如此长得令人难以置信的时间段，研究人员还必须考虑对地球气候的天文影响-即地球缓慢变化的轨道和向太阳倾斜如何影响在不同时间到达地球不同地区的太阳量，也就是众所周知的米兰科维奇周期(Milankovitch Cycle)。当研究小组将轨道数据与他们的同位素气候数据叠加时，他们看到轨道变化给全球气候带来了明显但相对较小的变化。研究人员说，至关重要的是，气候州之间的每一次大幅跃升都与温室气体水平的巨大变化有关。
例如，在恐龙灭绝约1000万年后，地球从暖房状态跃升为温室状态。扎霍斯说，这一被称为古新世-始新世最高温度的事件，温度比现代水平高出60华氏度(16摄氏度)，是由大量碳释放到大气中推动的，被认为是北大西洋大规模火山喷发的结果。同样，随着二氧化碳在接下来的2000万年里从大气中消失，南极洲开始形成冰原，地球进入酷宅阶段，表面平均温度比现代水平高出约40华氏度(4摄氏度)。
大约300万年前，在北半球冰盖消长的推动下，地球进入了冰屋阶段。现在，人类排放的温室气体正在导致气温上升到数千万年来未曾见过的程度。研究人员总结说，这种上升远远超出了地球改变轨道所引发的自然变化。如果目前的温室气体排放量保持稳定，气候可能会飙升至古新世-始新世最高温度以来未曾见过的水平。Zachos说，从冰库到温室的转变不会花费数百万年的时间，而需要数百年的时间。
该研究的主要作者、德国不来梅大学海洋环境科学中心主任托马斯·韦斯特霍尔德在声明中说，我们现在更准确地知道地球上什么时候变暖或变冷，对潜在的动力和驱动它们的过程有了更好的理解。特别令人感兴趣的是6600万年前到3400万年前的时间，当时地球的温度比今天高得多，因为它代表着过去与未来人类活动变化可能导致的结果的平行。
古新世-始新世最高温度，比现代水平高出60华氏度(16摄氏度)。

note_en:
Sixty-six million years ago, after a   massive asteroid hit Earth with the explosive energy of roughly   1 billion nuclear bombs, a shroud of ash, dust and vaporized rock covered the sky and slowly rained down on the planet. As plant and animal species died  en masse, tiny undersea amoebas called forams continued to reproduce, building sturdy shells out of calcium and other deep-sea minerals, just as they had for hundreds of millions of years. When each foram inevitably died — pulverized into seabed sediment — they kept a little piece of Earth's ancient history alive in their fossilized shells.
 For decades, scientists have studied those shells, finding clues about the ancient Earth's ocean temperatures, its carbon budget and the composition of minerals spilling through the air and seas. Now, in a new study published today (Sept. 10) in the journal   Science, researchers have analyzed the chemical elements in thousands of foram samples to build the most detailed climate record of Earth ever — and it reveals just how dire our current climate situation is.
  The new paper, which comprises decades of deep-ocean drilling missions into a single record, details Earth's climate swings across the entire   Cenozoic era — the 66 million-year period that began with the death of the   dinosaurs and extends to the present epoch of human-induced   climate change. The results show how Earth transitioned through four distinct climate states — dubbed the Warmhouse, Hothouse, Coolhouse and Icehouse states — in response to changes in the planet's orbit,   greenhouse gas levels and the extent of polar ice sheets.
  The zig-zagging chart (shown above) ends with a sobering peak. According to the researchers, the current pace of anthropogenic   global warming far exceeds the natural climate fluctuations seen at any other point in the Cenozoic era, and has the potential to hyper-drive our planet out of a long icehouse phase into a searing hothouse state.
 "Now that we have succeeded in capturing the natural climate variability, we can see that the projected anthropogenic warming will be much greater than that," study co-author James Zachos, professor of Earth and planetary sciences at the University of California, Santa Cruz,   said in a statement. "The Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC)   projections for 2300 in the 'business-as-usual' scenario will potentially bring global temperature to a level the planet has not seen in 50 million years." (The IPCC is a United Nations group that assesses the science, risks and impacts of climate change on the planet.)
    To compile their new era-long climate map, the study authors examined fossil foram shells in deep-sea sediment cores — long tubes of rock, sediment and microbes — drilled from the world's oceans over the past several decades. Forams (short for foraminifera) are microscopic plankton whose oldest relatives appeared in the ocean close to a billion years ago; the deeper scientists dig into the seabed, the older the foram specimens they uncover.
 The ratios of   carbon and   oxygen isotopes (versions of elements) in foram shells hold critical climate information. The ratio between the oxygen isotopes oxygen-18 and oxygen-16, for example, can reveal how warm the surrounding water was when a foram constructed its shell; the higher the ratio, the colder the water. The ratio between carbon-13 and carbon-12 shows how much organic carbon was available for the microbes to eat; here, a higher ratio correlates with more greenhouse gases (like carbon dioxide) in the atmosphere.
 Because the team's climate record covers such an incredibly long time period, the researchers also had to consider astronomic impacts on the planet's climate — that is, how Earth's slowly changing orbit and tilt toward the sun impact the amount of sunlight reaching different parts of the planet at different times, also known as   Milankovitch cycles. When the team overlaid orbital data with their isotopic climate data, they saw that orbital variations created distinct but relatively small-scale changes to the global climate. Crucially, each big jump between climate states was tied to a massive shift in   greenhouse gas levels, the researchers said.
  For example, about 10 million years after the dinosaur extinction,   Earth jumped from a warmhouse state to a hothouse state. This event, known as the Paleocene-Eocene Thermal Maximum, saw temperatures up to 60 degrees Fahrenheit (16 degrees Celsius) above modern levels, Zachos said, and was driven by a massive release of carbon into the atmosphere, thought to be the result of huge volcanic eruptions in the North Atlantic. Similarly, as carbon dioxide disappeared from the atmosphere over the next 20 million years, ice sheets started to form in   Antarctica and the planet entered a coolhouse phase, with surface temperatures averaging about 40 F (4 C) above modern levels.
   About 3 million years ago, Earth entered an icehouse phase, driven by waxing and waning ice sheets in the Northern Hemisphere. Now, human greenhouse gas emissions are causing temperatures to rise to an extent not seen in tens of millions of years. This rise is well beyond the natural variations triggered by Earth's changing orbit, the researchers concluded. And if current greenhouse emissions hold steady, the climate could skyrocket back to levels not seen since the Paleocene-Eocene Thermal Maximum. The transition from icehouse to hothouse won't take millions of years, Zachos said — it will take hundreds.
 "We now know more accurately when it was warmer or colder on the planet and have a better understanding of the underlying dynamics and the processes that drive them," lead study author Thomas Westerhold, Director of the University of Bremen Center for Marine Environmental Sciences in Germany, said in the statement. "The time from 66 [million] to 34 million years ago, when the planet was significantly warmer than it is today, is of particular interest, as it represents a parallel in the past to what future anthropogenic change could lead to."
     Paleocene-Eocene Thermal Maximum, saw temperatures up to 60 degrees Fahrenheit (16 degrees Celsius) above modern levels

note:
一位前灭绝起义(XR)发言人离开了这个环保组织，转而为核能而战，因为她说这是应对气候危机的唯一方法。
锡安·莱茨在“每日邮报”上写道，她已经无法为该组织的一些主张辩护。
她补充说，兜售世界末日悲观的信息会疏远人，而且几乎没有提供积极的解决方案。
该组织将自己描述为一场国际非暴力公民抗命活动，自2018年成立以来，已经参与了一系列高调的抗议活动。
上周，它通过封锁印刷机和推迟发行，将矛头对准了英国报纸-它指责这些报纸未能报道气候变化。
莱茨周四为“每日邮报”和“每日电讯报”撰文，解释了她放弃XR、支持核能的决定。
她告诉“每日邮报”，她最初加入XR是因为XR传递的信息是倾听科学家的意见，代言人的角色给了她一个平台，让她谈论我真正觉得重要的事情。
然而，她说，在去年10月做客BBC的安德鲁·尼尔节目后，她开始重新考虑自己对该组织的支持。
她被问及联合创始人罗杰·哈勒姆(Roger Hallam)的说法，即科学预测本世纪将有60亿人死于气候变化-这是他向BBC的HARDtalk提出的说法。
莱茨女士说：这是一个引人注目的断言，但不幸的是，它也不是真的，或者肯定没有任何证据支持。对于任何认识我的人-甚至是普通的观众-都很明显，我陷入了一场公关噩梦。
我无法为这个数字辩护，但作为官方发言人，我也不能被看到谴责它。取而代之的是，我所能做的就是在摄影棚的炽热灯光下挣扎了好久，感觉就像是永无止境。
莱茨在21世纪初还是一名学生时就开始宣传环境问题。她表示，她也对XR告诉人们什么不应该做的做法表示怀疑，并兜售气候危机的解决办法是让时光倒流到更简单的时代。
她在“每日电讯报”上写道，那些主张我们都需要少花钱的活动家--就像她曾经做过的那样--不得不接受这种情况不会发生，转而寻求解决方案。
她表示，XR内部的许多人主张用可再生能源完全取代化石燃料，但这不是一个现实的选择，她倾向于一种务实的方法，而不是同龄人要求坚持过时的主流绿线的部落压力。
她说，当任何理性的、以证据为基础的方法表明，包括核能在内的战略是以所需的规模和速度压低排放的唯一现实解决方案时，绿色运动的很大一部分是根深蒂固的反核思维。
她否认自己正在做出180度大转弯，相反，她说这是寻找解决方案合乎逻辑的下一步，而不是更加大声地叫喊这个问题。
莱茨说，自那以后，她一直在活动组织英国环境进步(Environmental Progress UK)担任角色，该组织的活动包括支持萨福克的塞兹韦尔C核电站(Sizewell C)的建设。
核能计划成为英国未来能源战略的重要组成部分。
BBC环境分析师罗杰·哈拉宾(Roger Harrabin)表示，它的主要好处是有助于保持灯光亮起，同时几乎没有产生任何导致气候变暖的二氧化碳排放。
XR的一位发言人表示：在高风险的情况下，你会考虑最坏的情况，并根据最坏的情况进行计划，因为风险足够高，有理由认真对待风险。这是一种在所有行业全面采用的方法。科学上的不确定性总是被扭曲，以阻止基于风险的分析。
灭绝反抗运动一直认为，公众应该知道可信的科学家认为未来几十年气候和生态危机发展的最坏情况是什么。
note_en:
A former Extinction Rebellion (XR) spokeswoman left the environmental group to campaign for nuclear power because she says it is the only way to deal with the climate crisis.
 Zion Lights,  writing in the Daily Mail, also said that she had become unable to defend some of the group's claims.
 XR "peddle messages of doomsday gloom that alienate" and offer "little in the way of positive solutions", she added.
  It describes itself as an international "non-violent civil disobedience activist movement" and has been involved in a number of high-profile protests since it was formed in 2018.
  Last week it targeted UK newspapers - which it has accused of failing to report on climate change - by  blocking printing presses and delaying distribution.
  Ms Lights wrote articles for both the Daily Mail and the Daily Telegraph on Thursday explaining her decision to leave behind XR and support nuclear power.
 She told the Mail she initially joined XR because its message was "listen to the scientists" and the role of spokesperson gave her a platform "to talk about what I truly felt mattered".
 However, she says she began to rethink her support for the group after  an appearance on the BBC's Andrew Neil Show last October.
 She was asked about co-founder Roger Hallam's claim that science predicts six billion people will die this century due to climate change -  a claim that he made to BBC's HARDtalk.
 Ms Lights said: "It's a headline-grabbing assertion - but unfortunately, it's also not true, or certainly not backed up by any evidence. As was obvious to anyone who knows me - and even to the casual viewer - I was plunged into a PR nightmare.
 "I could not defend the number, but as the official spokesperson nor could I be seen to condemn it. All I could do, instead, was flounder under the hot glare of the studio lights for what felt like an eternity."
  Ms Lights, who began campaigning about the environment as a student in the early 2000s, said she also had doubts about XR's approach of telling people "what not to do" and "peddling the notion that the solution to the climate crisis was to turn back the clock to a simpler time".
 Writing in the Telegraph, she said the campaigners who argued that we needed to all live with less - as she once did - had to accept this was not going to happen "and look to solutions instead".
  She said that "many within XR argue in favour of replacing fossil fuels entirely with renewables" but this was not a realistic option and she favoured "a pragmatic approach, rather than peer-group tribal pressure to stick to an outdated mainstream green line".
 Much of the green movement was "steeped in an anti-nuclear mindset", she said, "when any rational, evidence-based approach shows that a strategy including nuclear energy is the only realistic solution to driving down emissions at the scale and speed required".
 She denied she was making a U-turn, instead saying it was a "logical next step" in looking for solutions rather than "shouting ever more loudly about the problem".
 Ms Lights said she has since taken a role at campaign group Environmental Progress UK, whose campaigns include supporting  the building of the Sizewell C nuclear power station in Suffolk.
 Nuclear power is planned to be a key part of the UK's future energy strategy.
 BBC environment analyst Roger Harrabin said its key benefit is it helps keep the lights on while producing hardly any of the CO2 emissions that are heating the climate.
  A spokesperson for XR said: "In a high stakes scenario you look at the worst-case and plan based on that, because the risks are high enough to warrant taking the risk seriously. It's an approach employed across the board in all industries. Scientific uncertainty has always been twisted to prevent risk-based analysis.
 "Extinction Rebellion has always taken the view that the public deserve to know what credible scientists believe is the worst-case scenario for the development of the climate and ecological crisis over the coming decades."
title: 灭绝反抗：前发言人说，核能是唯一的选择
title_en: Extinction Rebellion: Nuclear power 'only option' says former spokeswoman

每个人都想跳出框框去思考。这样就不会跳出框框思考问题了。
有时，我会用困在盒子里的人的想法进行开箱即用的思考。比如，我怎么才能把这个困在盒子里的想法从盒子…中解放出来呢？你知道吗，…。没关系。我来解释一下。
所以你可能听说过黑暗的网络包含告密者、自由思想者、激进的创新者等等，尽管你可能会时不时地遇到坏人。
因此，在对这一问题进行长篇评论之前，让我用一个常见的类比来说明它，但要稍作修改，以便跳出框框。
因此，在这种情况下(仅通过这个类比)，人们正在思考的大黑匣子是在黑暗的网络中。因此，要像别人告诉你的那样“跳出盒子”去思考，你可能不得不走进盒子里。请看上图：您确实看到的文本就是清晰地写在图像上的内容。但是你可能看不到的图片标签中的Alt文本写着“盒子里思维的范式转变”(The Paradigm Shift Of The Box In The Box)。
除非您在浏览器上设置了立即显示alt文本的设置，否则您不会发现这一点，除非您专门挖掘它。但是，那你为什么要这么做呢？你不知道它会有用。反正它是藏起来的。但是有一个完整的网络的行为就像我刚才提到的Alt Text。(显然是隐藏的网络)。
好的，所以你的老师、父母或营销大师(他们可能会从你那里受益)说，跳出框框思考是件好事。也许，像我一样，你认为你会听。但是老师有没有告诉过你：
你不仅仅局限于跳出框框去思考。这个盒子真的是你的玩具。来去自如。为什么要把自己限制在盒子外面的地方呢？还是放在盒子里？或者为什么不换成袋子、保险箱或板条箱呢？
“如果我告诉你，在这个岛上的某个地方有一个非常大的盒子，不管你想要什么，不管你想要什么，当你打开那个盒子时，它就会在那里。”--本·莱纳斯。
因为在这种情况下，我们认为暗网就是盒子(任何人都不应该进入的盒子)，所以我们现在可以通过进入盒子来进行跳出盒子的思考。
搜索引擎可以很容易地根据出现在Clearnet之前的其他内容来判断内容的原创性。但在这个时候，他们通常不知道暗网中存在着什么(很难爬行，不可靠，以及其他问题)。
在上面的冰山图片中(这是一个恰当而准确的例子)，你可以看到深色的网比透明的网大得多。
因此，从字面上讲，你可以从黑暗的网络中搜索很酷的想法和内容，然后把它们放到你的透明网络网站上。根据搜索引擎的说法，结果将是：你有了新的、真实的内容。
此外，由于来源(暗网)的原因，您的“真实”内容可能是唯一的(对于明网)。暗网作者通常会有不同的想法。这就是他们在那里的原因。
你可能认为我在告诉你从黑暗的网络上窃取内容。
达利过去常常从一个陌生的环境中获得他的绘画灵感：他的梦想。
虽然他的成功从何而来是值得怀疑的(确实，我们不能假设灵感或艺术质量)，但他肯定对灵感略知一二：激进的灵感来自一个我们不常去的地方*。
*(这就是为什么我是一个狂热的圣经读者，不再踏足教堂。在我看来，教堂是宗教黑暗网络的阴暗面，但圣经是与当权者背道而驰的“激进”书籍。而且，奇怪的是，大多数圣经都是黑色的，虽然里面有很多光。)。
因此，使用隐藏的网络可以给你带来以前你不知道的想法，因为它们不是你通过主流电视或主流互联网(或主流书籍)共同世界或共同体验的一部分。
正如我在本文开头所承认的那样，黑暗的网络包含坏人，尽管偶尔你可能会遇到告密者、自由思想者、激进的创新者等等。
除非作者有意，否则暗网的存在并不是为了可搜索。同样，你偶然发现真正邪恶内容的机会也取决于你使用的入口点。寻找创新将从创新开始。
因此，我建议不要仅仅根据流行的切入点跳入黑暗网络，而是要找到一种策略，从探索创新或受到主流观点挑战的新想法的有用的黑暗网站获得链接和洋葱连接。把你的眼睛从真正黑暗的东西上移开。
网站是这样，人也是这样：物以类聚，物以类聚。因此，如果你从那些具有知识性和进步性的网站开始，你不太可能很快进入邪恶的领域。另一方面，如果你从一个像隐藏维基…这样的网站开始。你最好知道你在做什么。
其实读经文要好得多。最好的灵感来自人们很少去的地方。这会让你知道你不知道你不了解…。(已存档)
note_en:
Everyone wants to "think out of the box". So that's not thinking out of the box.
 Sometimes I think out of the box using the ideas from the people stuck in the box. Like, how can I take this idea that is stuck in the box, and liberate it from the box…you know what…nevermind. I’ll just explain.
   So you may have heard that the dark web contains whistle blowers, free thinkers, radical innovators, and such like these, though you might run into a bad guy every now and then.
 So before drawing out a large commentary on this subject, let me illustrate it with a common analogy, but slightly modified so as to exit the box.
  So in this case (and by this analogy only) the big dark box that people are thinking in is in the dark web. So to think “out of the box” as you’ve been told to do, you might have to  go into the box. Consider this picture above: The   text you do see is what is clearly written on the image. But the ALT TEXT in the image tag which you will probably not see says  “The Paradigm Shift of Thinking in the Box”.
 Unless you have a setting on your browser that shows alt text immediately, you wouldn’t discover this unless you dug for it, specifically. But then, why would you do that? You didn’t know it would be useful. It was hidden anyways. But there is an entire network that behaves like that Alt Text I just mentioned. (The hidden web, obviously).
  Ok, so your teacher or parents or marketing guru (who could benefit by you) said its a good thing to think out of the box. And maybe, like me, you thought you’d listen. But did the teacher ever tell you to:
  Your not limited to thinking only outside the box. The box is really your plaything. Come and go at will. Why limit yourself to the place outside the box? Or in the box? Or why not switch to a bag, safe, or crate?
   “What if I told you that somewhere on this island there’s a very large box and whatever you imagined, whatever you wanted to be in it, when you opened that box there it would be.”” -  Ben Linus
 Since in this case we are considering the dark web as being the box (the box that NOBODY SHOULD ENTER) we can now think out of the box by going into the box.
  Search engines can easily judge the originality of content based on other content that appeared before it on the  clearnet. But at this time they  generally don’t know what exists in the dark net (its hard to crawl, not reliable, among other issues).
 In the ice-berg picture above (which is a fitting and accurate example) you can see that the dark net is far bigger than the clear net.
 So you could, literally,   just search for cool ideas and content from the dark web and place it on your clear web website. And the result would be, according to search engines:  You  have new and authentic content.
 Adding to that, due to the source (the dark net), your “authentic” content will probably be unique (to the clear net). Dark net authors often think differently. That’s why they are there.
 You probably think I’m telling you to steal content from the dark web.
       Dali used to take inspiration for his paintings from a foreign and strange environment:   his dreams.
 While its questionable where his success came from (indeed, we cannot assume inspiration or quality of art) he certainly knew a thing or two about inspiration: Radical inspiration comes from a place we don’t visit often*.
 *( That’s why I’m an avid bible reader that no longer sets foot in churches. Churches are, in my opinion, the dark side of the dark web of religion, but the bible is the “radical” book that goes against the establishment. And, oddly, most bibles come in black, though containing much light.).
 Hence, using the   hidden net can give you ideas that were previously unknown to you, because they are not a part of your common world or common experiences via mainstream television or mainstream internet (or mainstream books).
    Like I acknowledged at the beginning of this article, the dark web contains bad guys, though occasionally you might run into whistle blowers, free thinkers, radical innovators, and the like.
 The dark web does not exist to be searchable, unless the authors intend it. Likewise, your chances on stumbling upon genuinely evil content is also based on the entry points you use. Finding innovation will start with innovation.
 Hence, I would suggest NOT just jumping into the dark web based on the popular entry points, but find a strategy to obtain links and onion connections from useful darkweb sites that explore innovation or new ideas that are challenged by dominant opinion. Avert your eyes from things that are genuinely dark.
 Its true with websites as its true with people: Birds of a feather flock together. So if you start with sites that are intellectual and progressive in nature, you are not likely to come into evil territories quickly. On the other hand, if you start with a site like The Hidden Wiki…you’d better know what your doing.
     Its actually much better to read the scriptures. The best inspiration comes from places people rarely visit.  That would reveal much to you that you didn’t know you didn’t know… ( archived)
title: 使用来自Dark Web的内容向Clear Web提供内容
title_en: Using Content from the Dark Web to Provide Content to the Clear Web

圣地亚哥备受争议的智能路灯摄像头将被关闭，这赢得了社会正义活动家的掌声，但警方无法使用他们所说的有助于解决暴力犯罪的工具。
周三，市长凯文·福尔科纳(Kevin Faulconer)下令关闭安装在圣地亚哥各地路灯上的3000多个摄像头，直到该市制定一项管理监控技术的法令。
市长发言人古斯塔沃·波特拉(Gustavo Portela)表示，福尔科纳是在市议会考虑使用这种技术之前，“根据市议员和社区领袖的明确反馈”发出这一呼吁的，他们敦促在市议会考虑使用这种技术之前，制定一项监控条例。
在这一出人意料的声明发布的几天前，该市提出了一项计划，将摄像头管理和访问权限完全交给警方-该计划因缺乏公众监督而立即遭到社区角落的抵制。在福尔科纳的决定宣布前几个小时，该市对该计划踩下了刹车-这也是一个惊喜。
拔掉摄像头的插头是圣地亚哥价值3000万美元的Smart路灯短暂但曲折历史上的最新转折。该项目最初是在2016年向公众介绍的，当时是一项升级LED灯的计划，以节省资金和能源。
但智能路灯不仅仅是灯光。它们包括安装在路灯上的摄像头和技术先进的传感器，能够将汽车和人的视频图像转化为城市可以使用的有价值的数据-因此智能路灯是“智能”的。
数据已经收集，但该市认为可能会出现的用途从未实现。然而，虽然这不是最初计划的一部分，但警方后来开始审查原始摄像机镜头，以帮助解决严重或暴力犯罪。
去年，人们意识到街灯里有摄像头。争论爆发了。活动人士担心可能会有监控和侵犯民权的行为，以及有色人种社区的过度监管，他们希望在该市建立透明、监督和问责的方法之前，关闭摄像头。
周三，担心摄像头的团体联盟成员热内维夫·琼斯-赖特(Geneviéve Jones-Wright)表示，目前关闭摄像头的决定意味着社区获得了它所寻求的东西。
她在一封电子邮件中写道：“永远不要低估以(D)法治的名义走到一起的社区成员的力量。”“有工作要做，我们仍保持警惕。我们不会下岗。“。
几个月来，该市一直在努力起草监控监督条例。目前还不清楚什么时候可以准备好。
停止使用摄像头的决定也是在圣地亚哥和拥有特殊传感器背后技术的Ubiquia公司试图敲定一份新合同的同时做出的。
该市已经几个月没有从智能路灯收集机动性或环境数据了。当合同在6月份结束时，Ubiquia关闭了传感器。
但Ubiquia确实同意保持摄像头运转，这样警方就可以在需要时获取录像。
上周，该市提出了一项计划：数据收集成本大于收益，因此也许应该关闭传感器，摄像头管理可以移交给该市警察局。
这些摄像头对警方很有用，在过去的两年里，他们至少访问了400次原始镜头。他们称赞这些摄像头是游戏规则的改变者，并表示这些图像有助于识别去年在奥泰梅萨教堂鸡肉餐厅开枪打死一名员工的枪手嫌疑人。
一名警官上周表示，摄像头是如此重要的资产，该部门愿意承担未来四年运营摄像头的700万美元成本。
但当该计划公布时，活动称其为“音盲”，特别是在反对警察偏见和种族不公正的抗议活动扰乱该地区和国家的情况下。
周三，负责管理智能路灯的可持续发展部的负责人计划前往该市公共安全与宜居社区委员会(Public Security&Amp；Living Neighborhood Committee)，介绍移交计划。取而代之的是，部门主任科迪·霍芬要求委员会将该项目从议程中删除。她说，该市将“进一步评估我们的选择”。
在该项目被从议程中删除后不久，但在福尔科纳宣布之前，领导公共安全和宜居邻里委员会的委员会成员赞扬了胡芬推迟的决定。
在一份书面声明中，女议员莫妮卡·蒙哥马利·斯特普(Monica Montgomery Steppe)表示，问题“不在于监控技术是否可以用作公共安全工具。问题在于SDPD内部的透明度。“。
她说：“此时此刻，我们做出的每一个决定，要么与社区建立信任，要么摧毁信任。”她说，监督到位是“当务之急”。“在这个框架到位之前，不应该做出任何决定。”
警方将对关闭摄像头决定的评论提交给市长办公室。
note_en:
San Diego’s controversial Smart Streetlight cameras will be shut off, drawing applause from social justice activists, but removing police access to a tool they say helps solve violent crimes.
 Mayor Kevin Faulconer on Wednesday ordered that the more than 3,000 cameras installed on streetlights throughout San Diego be turned off until the city crafts an ordinance to govern surveillance technology.
 Mayoral spokesman Gustavo Portela said Faulconer made the call “following clear feedback” from council members and community leaders, who pressed to get a surveillance ordinance in place before the City Council considers using such technology.
 The surprise announcement came days after the city floated a plan to hand over the camera management and access exclusively to police — a plan that drew immediate pushback from corners of the community for its lack of public oversight. The city hit the brakes on that plan hours before Faulconer’s decision was announced — also a surprise.
    Pulling the plug on the cameras is the latest turn in the short but twisty history of San Diego’s $30 million Smart Streetlights. The project was initially introduced to the public in 2016 as a plan to upgrade LED lights to save money and energy.
 But the Smart Streetlights were more than lights. They included streetlight-mounted cameras and technologically advanced sensors with the ability to turn video images of cars and people into valuable data the city could use — thus the “smart” in Smart Streetlights.
 The data was collected, but the uses the city thought might follow never materialized. However, while it was not part of the initial plan, police later began to review the raw camera footage to help solve serious or violent crime.
    Then last year, people realized there were cameras in the streetlights. Controversy erupted. Activists raised fears of potential surveillance and civil rights abuses, and over-policing in communities of color, and they wanted the cameras off until the city established methods for transparency, oversight and accountability.
 On Wednesday, Geneviéve Jones-Wright, a member of a coalition of groups concerned about the cameras, said the decision to turn them off for now means the community won what it had sought.
 “Never underestimate the power of community members who come together in the name of (d)emocracy,” she wrote in an email. “There is work to be done and we remain vigilant. We will not get off our posts.”
 The city has been working to craft surveillance oversight ordinances for several months. It is not clear when one might be ready.
  The decision to stop using the cameras also comes as San Diego and Ubicquia, the company that owns the technology behind the special sensors, try to hammer out a new contract.
 The city has not collected mobility or environmental data from the Smart Streetlights for months. Ubicquia turned the sensors off when the contract ended in June.
 But Ubicquia did agree to keep the cameras rolling so police could access footage if needed.
 Last week, the city pitched a plan: The data-collection costs outweighed the benefits, so maybe the sensors should stay off, and the camera management could be handed over to the city’s Police Department.
  The cameras were useful to police, who accessed the raw footage at least 400 times in the last two years. They hailed the cameras as a game-changer, and said the images helped to identify the suspected gunman who shot three employees, killing one, last year at an Otay Mesa Church’s Chicken.
 One police official said last week that the cameras are such an important asset, the department was willing to absorb the $7 million cost to run them for the next four years.
 But when the plan was made public, activities called it “tone deaf,” especially as it comes as the protests against police bias and racial injustice roil the region and the country.
 On Wednesday, the head of the Sustainability Department, which runs the Smart Streetlights, was slated to go in front of the city’s Public Safety & Livable Neighborhoods Committee and pitch the hand-off plan. Instead, Department Director Cody Hooven asked the committee to pull the item from its agenda. She said the city would “further assess our options.”
  Shortly after the item was pulled from the agenda, but before Faulconer’s announcement, the council member who heads the Public Safety & Livable Neighbhorhoods Committee praised Hooven’s decision to hold off.
 In a written statement, Councilwoman Monica Montgomery Steppe said the issue “is not whether surveillance technology can be used as a public safety tool. The issue is transparency within SDPD.”
 “Every decision that we make, in this moment, will either build trust with the community or tear it down,” she said, saying it is “imperative” to have oversight in place. “No decision should be made until that framework is in place.”
 Police referred comment on the decision to turn off the cameras to the mayor’s office.
title: 圣地亚哥路灯上的摄像头在法令实施前关闭
title_en: Cameras on San Diego streetlights turned off until ordinance in place

note:
周三，旧金山世界末日般的橙色天空成为了全城的话题-而且远远超出了这个话题。然而，许多人发现他们捕捉超现实图像的努力受阻，因为他们的iPhone将烟雾弥漫的天空修正为更自然的色调。
最重要的是：智能手机相机在许多情况下都表现出色，这要归功于一款软件，它会自动尝试改善镜头的构图、对焦和诸如白与色平衡等设置。但这些调整也可能会阻碍捕捉一些生活中最生动的形象的独特之处。
在橙色的天空中醒来后，我首先试图从后门拍摄，但发现我的iPhone正在将天空调整为更常见的灰色。在社交媒体上，我看到很多人都有同样的体验，手机上的静止和视频都是如此。
中午左右，我带着一部iPhone 11 Pro Max、一部Pixel 4a、一部Galaxy Note 20和我的佳能单反，前往伯纳尔高地公园(Beral Heights Park)，那里可以俯瞰整个城市，包括旧金山市中心和海湾大桥(Bay Bridge)。
Galaxy Note 20在捕捉天空的鲜艳色调方面做得最好(见上文)，但没有一款手机能与我用佳能捕捉到的效果相提并论。
我的iPhone能够捕捉到天空的色调的唯一一张照片还包括我们的橙色本田飞度(Honda Fit)。
是的，但是：在所有情况下，我都使用该设备的默认设置。彭博社记者莎拉·弗里尔说，她使用Hallide应用程序是为了避免iPhone的色彩校正。
归根结底：这是我的佳能证明了一个时刻，尽管它体积庞大，但并不总是能被智能手机取代。
这是iPhone能够显示橙色天空的照片：
note_en:
The apocalyptic orange sky in San Francisco Wednesday was the talk of the town — and well beyond. However, many people found their efforts to capture the surreal images stymied, as their iPhones "corrected" the smoke-filled sky to a more natural hue.
 The big picture: Smartphone cameras do a great job in many situations thanks to software that automatically tries to improve a shot's composition, focus, and settings like white and color balance. But those adjustments can also get in the way of capturing what's unique about some of life's most vivid images.
 After waking up to the orange sky, I first tried to shoot out my back door, but found my iPhone was adjusting the sky to a much more common gray. On social media, I saw lots of others  having the same experience with both still and video coming from their phones.
 Around midday, I headed to Bernal Heights Park, which overlooks the city, including downtown SF and the Bay Bridge, armed with an iPhone 11 Pro Max, a Pixel 4a, a Galaxy Note 20 and my Canon DSLR.
 The Galaxy Note 20 did the best job of the smartphones (see above) at capturing the vivid hues of the sky, but none of the phones came close to what I was able to capture using the Canon.
 The one shot where my iPhone was able to capture the sky's hue also included our orange Honda Fit.
 Yes, but: In all cases I used the device's default settings. Bloomberg reporter Sarah Frier said she  used the app Hallide to avoid the iPhone's color correction.
 The bottom line: This was a moment for my Canon to prove that, despite its bulk, it can't always be replaced by a smartphone.
   And here is the photo where the iPhone was able to show the orange sky:
title: 智能手机相机努力捕捉旧金山橙色的天空
title_en: Smartphone cameras struggle to capture San Francisco's orange sky

note:
通过足智多谋和力量，孟买人克服了各种障碍和困难。
“我很羡慕写这本书的年轻人。我也有一些追求冒险的红色血液，一些甚至驱使一个人前进的旅行欲望。但是命运和环境阻止了我用普通的方式来满足它--我通过其他方式寻求冒险。“。
贾瓦哈拉尔·尼赫鲁(Jawaharlal Nehru)为三名20世纪20年代骑车环游世界的帕西族人写的一本书的序言只是其中令人愉快的事情之一。
书中一章又一章地描述了这些年轻人的嬉戏冒险，比如其中一个人被锤子砸碎了胸口的石头，而另一个人则用牙齿夹着绳子拖着一辆车。
阿努普·巴巴尼(Anoop Babani)带着乐趣和兴趣与世界各地的自行车手一起阅读了这本书，但让这位前记者和自行车手感兴趣的是书的结尾。
在“也许是最后一页”，上面写着1928年3月，三个帕西族人回到孟买时受到的盛大接待，希望“另一个要回来的家伙”也会受到盛大的接待。
这家伙是谁？他什么时候离开印度，什么时候回来的？巴巴尼必须找出真相。
他的研究显示，在20世纪20年代和30年代，10名印度人-都是20多岁的年轻人，都是帕西族人，都来自孟买-选择分成三个不同的小组骑自行车环游世界。
“由于强烈渴望将印度母亲的名字带到世界上遥远的角落，”他们穿越了山脉、森林和平原，有时没有食物和水。最终，他们中只有7人完成了他们的奥德赛之旅。
第一组骑车出发的是孟买举重俱乐部的六名成员。他们是Adi Hakim，Jal Bapasola，Rustom Bhumgara，Gustad Hathiram，Keki Pochkhanawala和Nariman Kapadia。
根据Rustom Bhumgara的儿子Rohinton Bhumgara的说法，这六个年轻人在1920年参加了一位从欧洲步行到印度的法国人的公开讲座。听他讲话使他们深受鼓舞。
他们的旅程始于1923年10月，途经旁遮普、俾路支省、中东、欧洲、美国、日本和东南亚。
途中，一名队员因“个人原因”从德黑兰返回印度，另外两名队员对美国非常“迷恋”，因此留了下来。
巴帕索拉82岁的儿子诺希尔·巴帕索拉(Noshir Bapasola)告诉巴巴尼：“有一次，他(贾尔·巴帕索拉)讲述了他们是如何与位于孟买的英国罗利循环公司(Raleigh Cycle Co Of England)就赞助自行车一事进行接触的。”诺希尔·巴帕索拉住在新泽西州。
“公司拒绝了。但当他们到达英国时，他说公司恳求他们使用他们的自行车。他问他们为什么改变主意，被相当直截了当地告知‘我们不相信你们这些孩子会这么成功’。“。
到1928年3月，哈基姆、巴帕索拉和邦加拉到达印度时，他们已经行驶了大约7万公里。
在“世界各地骑自行车的人”一书中，他们以“无可厚非的骄傲”列举了他们的成就：在四年半的时间里，他们攀登了阿尔卑斯山，越过了“海盗出没的地区”，与“敌对的半野蛮部落”一起涉水穿越丛林，有时还“死里逃生”。
反过来，他们的努力又激励了一位来自孟买的体育记者弗拉姆罗兹·达瓦尔(Framroze Davar)骑着自行车出发，一切都是自己的。
9个月后，5000公里后，在维也纳，“他遇到了古斯塔夫·什塔夫雅尼克(Gustav Sztavjanik)，一位骑自行车的人，他的旅程给他留下了深刻的印象，”巴巴尼说。他加入了达瓦尔，他们都骑自行车环游世界长达7年之久。他们的旅程是最长、最艰难、最冒险的。“。
Sztavjanik和Davar骑马越过阿尔卑斯山，包括勃朗峰、苏联部分地区、波罗的海国家和斯堪的纳维亚半岛，然后转向法国。
从那里，他们航行到非洲的阿尔及尔，在撒哈拉和亚马逊森林中骑自行车，背着自行车-每辆重约15公斤-爬上安第斯山脉5200米。
根据巴巴尼的说法，他们的苦难永无止境。他们面对了八场沙尘暴，水都用完了。地形太多刺了，他们不得不在轮胎里填满草。他们甚至感染了疟疾。
然而，他们面临的最大挑战是亚马逊。在茂密的森林中挣扎着骑自行车，他们被迫建造了木筏。谢天谢地，一个猎头部落为他们提供了庇护所。
奥地利作家赫尔曼·哈特尔(Hermann Härtel)在一本关于什塔夫雅尼克的书中写道：“这是他们有史以来第一次从西海岸到南美东海岸进行这样的旅行，花了大约9个月的时间。”“这是一个未知的领域，非常危险。在他们之前的许多探险家再也没有回来。“
当这两个人到达缅甸时，他们发现自己置身于一群野生大象之中。Sztavjanik受伤，住院一个月。
达瓦尔总共穿越了10,000公里，52个国家和5大洲，他写了三本关于他的旅行的书：在世界屋脊上骑自行车，在现实和浪漫中穿越撒哈拉和亚马逊。
巴巴尼说：“这些书是人类学的伟大研究成果。”在书中，达瓦尔谈到了他在亚马逊遇到的部落，“他们的习俗，他们的样子，他们的衣服和他们狩猎的东西”。
1933年，这些冒险的故事启发了第三批帕西族人-凯基·哈拉斯(Keki Kharas)、鲁斯塔姆·甘地(Rustam Ghandhi)和鲁顿·史洛夫(Rutton Shroff)-骑自行车环游世界。
在九年时间里横跨五大洲8.4万公里之后，他们与人合著了两本书：骑车穿越阿富汗荒野和穿越世界高速公路。
在第一篇文章中，他们写道：“在阿富汗，我们连续三天三夜被困在沙漠中，没有食物，也没有水……”
对于这些自行车手来说，探险不是为了挑战他们的体能和智力，而是为了向世界展示印度。
“他们就像是品牌大使，”巴巴尼说，他去年12月在果阿马尔高的Ravindra Bhavan的一个展览上展示了他的研究成果。
在名为“我们的马鞍，我们的屁股，他们的世界”的展览上，巴巴尼展示了一辆自行车，其中一部分是在西孟加拉邦阿桑索尔附近的森-罗利自行车工厂生产的。它于1950年开业，是印度最早的自行车制造工厂之一。
在此之前，自行车必须进口，这就是为什么只有包括哈基姆公司在内的富人才能买得起。
巴巴尼计划写一本关于帕西族人的旅行和印度自行车文化背后的历史的书。这本名为“兜售历史：简陋自行车的兴衰与崛起”的书将于今年出版。
当被问及这些勇敢的骑自行车的人最让他印象深刻的是什么时，巴巴尼说，是他们的“真正的纯真”：“这打动了我的心灵，激励我去研究他们的旅程并写作。”
标题图片：美国纽约的Keki Kharas，Rustam Ghandhi和Rutton Shroff。|礼貌：阿努普·巴巴尼(Anoop Babani)
note_en:
Through resourcefulness and strength, the men from Bombay overcame all kinds of obstacles and hardships.
    “I envy the young men who have made the book. I too have some of the red blood which seeks adventure; some thing of the wanderlust which even drives one forward. But fate and circumstances have prevented from satisfying it in the ordinary way – I seek adventure in other ways.”
 Jawaharlal Nehru’s foreword to a book  by three Parsi men who cycled around the world in the 1920s was just one of the delightful things about it.
 Chapter after chapter in the book describes the young men’s rollicking adventures, like the time one of them got stones broken on his chest with a hammer while another dragged a car with a rope held between his teeth.
 Anoop Babani read the book,   With Cyclists Around The World, with amusement and interest, but it was the end that intrigued the former journalist and cyclist.
 There, on “perhaps the last page”, was the hope that the grand reception received by the three Parsis upon their return to Bombay in March 1928 would also be accorded to “another fellow who is coming back”.
 Who was this fellow? When did he leave India and when did he come back? Babani had to find out.
 His research revealed that in the 1920s and 1930s, 10 Indians – all in their 20s, all Parsis, and all from Bombay – chose to cycle around the globe in three separate groups.
 “Fired by the intense desire to carry the name of the country – Mother India – to the far-flung corners of the world,” they journeyed across mountains, forests and plains, sometimes without food and water. Eventually, only seven of them completed their odyssey.
  The first group to set off on their bicycles was made up of six members of the Bombay Weightlifting Club. They were Adi Hakim, Jal Bapasola, Rustom Bhumgara, Gustad Hathiram, Keki Pochkhanawala and Nariman Kapadia.
 According to Rohinton Bhumgara, son of Rustom Bhumgara, the six youngsters had attended a public lecture in 1920 by a Frenchman who had walked from Europe to India. Hearing him talk left them deeply inspired.
 Their journey began in October 1923 and meandered through Punjab, Balochistan, the Middle East, Europe, United States, Japan and South East Asia.
 On the way, one team member returned to India from Tehran for “personal reasons”, while two others were so “enamoured” of America that they stayed back.
   “Once, he [Jal Bapasola] narrated how they approached the Raleigh Cycle Co of England in Bombay about [the company] sponsoring the cycles,” Babani was told by Bapasola’s 82-year-old son Noshir Bapasola, who lives in New Jersey.
 “The company refused. But when they reached England, he said the company was begging them to use their cycles. He asked them why they had a change of heart and was told quite bluntly ‘we did not believe that you boys would be so successful’.”
 By the time Hakim, Bapasola and Bhumgara reached India in March 1928, they had covered around 70,000 kilometres.
 In their book  With Cyclists Around The World, they enumerated their achievements with “pardonable pride”: in four and a half years, they had scaled the Alps, crossed “pirate-infested territories” and waded through jungles with “hostile semi-savage tribes”, sometimes “escaping death by inches”.
  Their efforts, in turn, inspired a sports journalist from Bombay named Framroze Davar to set off on his bicycle, all on his own.
 Nine months and 5,000 kilometres later, in Vienna, “he met Gustav Sztavjanik, [a cyclist] who was impressed with his journey,” said Babani. “He joined Davar and they both cycled around the world for seven years. Theirs was the longest, toughest and most adventurous journey.”
     Sztavjanik and Davar rode over the Alps, including Mont Blanc, parts of the Soviet Union, the Baltic states and Scandinavia, before turning towards France.
 From there, they sailed to Algiers in Africa, pedalled in the Sahara and the Amazon forests, and climbed 5,200 metres up the Andes with their cycles – each weighing around 15 kilograms – on their backs.
 According to Babani, their hardships knew no end. They faced eight sandstorms and ran out of water. The terrain was so thorny that they had to stuff their tyres with grass. They even contracted malaria.
 The biggest challenge for them, though, was the Amazon. Struggling to cycle in the thick forest, they were forced to build rafts. Thankfully for them, a tribe of head-hunters gave them shelter.
 “It was their first such trip ever from the West coast to the East coast of South America and took them about nine months,” wrote Austrian author Hermann Härtel in a book on Sztavjanik. “This was uncharted territory and very dangerous. Many explorers before them never made it back out again.”
   When the two reached Burma, they found themselves amidst a herd of wild elephants. Sztavjanik was injured and hospitalised for a month.
 Davar, who covered a total of 10,000 kilometres, 52 countries and five continents, wrote three books on his travels:  Cycling Over Roof Of The World,  Across The Sahara and  The Amazon in Reality and Romance.
 “These books are a great study in anthropology,” said Babani. In them, Davar talks about the tribes he met in the Amazon, “their customs, how they look, their clothing and what they hunt”.
 Stories of these adventures inspired a third group of Parsi men – Keki Kharas, Rustam Ghandhi and Rutton Shroff – to cycle around the world in 1933.
 After covering 84,000 kilometres spanning five continents over nine years, they co-authored two books:  Pedaling Through The Afghan Wilds and  Across The Highways Of The World.
 In the first, they wrote, “In Afghanistan, we were marooned in the desert for three successive days and nights without either food or water....”
        For these cyclists the expeditions were not about challenging their physical and mental ability, but to present India to the world.
 “They were like brand ambassadors,” said Babani, who displayed his research at an exhibition at Ravindra Bhavan in Margao, Goa, in December.
 At the exhibition, titled  Our Saddles, Our Butts, Their World, Babani showed a cycle, a part of which was manufactured at the Sen-Raleigh bicycle factory near Asansol in West Bengal. Opened in 1950, it was one of the earliest bicycle manufacturing units in India.
 Prior to that, bicycles had to be imported, which is why only the wealthy, including Hakim and Co., could afford them.
 Babani plans to write a book on the Parsi men’s journeys and the history behind India’s cycling culture. Titled  Peddling History: Rise, Fall and Rise of Humble Bikes, the book will be out this year.
 When asked what struck him most about the intrepid cyclists, Babani said, it was their “genuine innocence”: “That’s what touched my mind and inspired me to do research about their journeys and to write.”
     Header image: Keki Kharas, Rustam Ghandhi and Rutton Shroff in New York, USA. | Courtesy: Anoop Babani
title: 一个世纪前骑自行车环游世界的7名帕西族人的冒险经历
title_en: The adventures of 7 Parsi men who cycled across the world century ago

YC W21批次将是远程的。在全球大流行的情况下，我们创始人和员工的安全是我们的首要任务。
S20是我们的第一批远程批次。作为YC初创公司的创始人和YC合伙人，我个人经历了过去18批中的16批，我非常有信心S20对我们的公司来说是一次变革性的经历。
在过去的六个月里，远程操作给了我们构建大量软件的机会。我们已经改进了YC创始人入职、校友名录、我们的内部维基、我们的创业建议目录和Demo Day网站。我们还集成了Slake和Zoom进行聊天和视频会议。从本质上讲，YC是一家软件公司，远程操作给了我们机会，让我们在自己最擅长的事情上加倍努力。
由于这些改进，YC合作伙伴能够与超过15个时区的公司合作，完成的个人和团队办公时间比之前的批次多50%(总计3600小时)，并与所有团队实时聊天(批次期间发送了超过250,000条Slack消息)。最后，YC虚拟演示日非常成功，表现优于过去几年的许多面对面演示日(投资者和创始人之间进行了超过28,000次介绍)。我们预计W21也会同样成功。
申请W21批次的截止时间是9月23日太平洋时间晚上8点。我们期待着阅读您的申请。
note_en:
YC W21 batch will be remote. Amid the global pandemic, the safety of our founders and staff is our top priority.
 S20 was our first remote batch. I’ve personally experienced 16 of the last 18 batches as a YC startup founder and then YC partner, and I’m very confident S20 was a transformative experience for our companies.
 Going remote gave us the opportunity to build a lot of software over the past six months. We’ve improved YC founder onboarding, alumni directories, our internal wiki, our catalogue of startup advice, and the Demo Day website. We also integrated Slack and Zoom for chat and video conferencing. At its heart, YC is a software company and going remote has given us the opportunity to double down on what we do best.
 As a result of these improvements, YC partners were able to work with companies in over 15 time zones, complete 50% more individual and group office hours than in previous batches (3600 hours in total), and chat with all of the teams in realtime (over 250,000 Slack messages were sent during the batch). Finally, YC Virtual Demo Day was extremely successful and out performed many in person Demo Days from years past (over 28,000 introductions were made between investors and founders). We expect W21 to be just as successful.
 The deadline for  applying to the W21 batch is September 23 at 8:00pm PT. We look forward to reading your application.
title: YC W21远程批次

note:
自从我16岁时拿着一张似是而非的ModRetro名片，除了最终成为Oculus Rift的第一个原型之外，几乎没有什么科技行业经验，自从被禁止参加消费电子展(CES)以来，我每年都会去参加消费电子展(CES)。虽然安杜里尔不生产消费电子产品，但我仍然每年都会参加，探索新技术，会见朋友、合作伙伴和供应商，他们勇敢地面对拉斯维加斯的人群，寻找深奥的技术。
12年来，我们在CES上目睹了无人机技术的出现，然后又见证了繁荣，这充分说明了一件事：中国赢了。美国公司建造了很多很酷的科博会项目，这些项目得到了很多媒体的关注，但在现实世界中的部署却很少--从灯光秀到送餐。“我们也设计了很多有趣的小批量消费无人机，但大多数都严重依赖中国的研发，然后他们的产品就在中国制造。是的，我知道，我完全知道我几年前就对Oculus做了同样的事情。”
如果中国只是赢得了销售游戏，这种情况就不会让我那么恼火了。作为一名电脑游戏玩家，我深深地理解，考虑到它们的易用性和价格，更便宜的选择(游戏机)卖得很好。然而，就无人机而言，中国在整个领域都处于全球无人机行业的领先地位，不仅在数量上，而且在质量上！大疆通过建造令人难以置信的成像、网络、电力和航空系统而主导着高端市场，而数百家位于深圳的公司则被起重机紧随其后。以及大多数由西方公司设计的产品。DJI可能会在这里和那里被一些专注于特定功能或规格的新贵击败，但当谈到整体套餐时，他们甚至很少接近。当涉及到消费者时，整体套餐才是最重要的。
可悲的是，世界各地的军队也是如此，包括美国和我们的盟友。考虑到最新的消费无人机技术提供了惊人的能力，许多拥有任何形式的采购权限的单位都放弃了价格高达数十万美元的美国制造的无人机，转而选择了只需几百美元的中国无人机，原因是他们希望体积小、画质好、使用方便(特别是在起飞和降落期间)，以及能够在原地盘旋，而不是在不断变化的视角周围飞行轨道(圈)。这激发了相当多美国无人机初创公司善意但半心半意的尝试，试图将业余爱好者和消费者无人机设计应用于军队。针对消费者和业余爱好者的需求进行优化的设计，对于勇敢的用户来说很少是正确的答案，他们花费更多，要求更多，从安全性到耐用性，再到耐力等等。换句话说，服务于一线的男男女女。
这就是我们建造Ghost的原因。它是美国设计的，美国制造的，是为世界上要求最高的用户-战士的需求而从头开始建造的。“我们抛弃了每个人都使用了十年的模板，从第一原则开始，用世界级的设计制造了一些非凡的东西。我们没有发明任何新的化学、冶金或物理，我们只是以一种非常新的方式使用了每个人都可以使用的相同材料。现在，战士们已经使用以前版本的Ghost已经有一段时间了，我们没有发明任何新的化学、冶金或物理。我们只是以一种非常新的方式使用了同样的材料。现在，战士们已经使用了以前版本的Ghost已经有一段时间了，但随着我们推出Ghost 4，我们终于可以更公开地谈论我们在这个领域的工作了。
我不可能涵盖“幽灵”中使其成为最好的无人机的所有元素(反正一个人可以携带--捕食者不算)，因为要覆盖的东西太多了。最好的尝试方式可能是与人们熟悉的其他无人机的几个元素进行比较：
幽灵是一种单旋翼飞机。旋翼系统的机械结构比DJI型四轴飞行器更难设计和制造，飞行控制也更难编程，但其结果是空气动力学磁盘负载要低得多，这导致了更长的耐力(在实际任务有效载荷下超过100分钟)、近乎无声的声学特征、高最大有效载荷能力(几十磅)和高速。相比之下，DJI Inspire 2是一款售价3000美元的高端产品，可以与一对夫妇一起笨拙地行走。我们可以在没有任何东西的情况下驾驶无人机更长时间，但这不是我的风格。对于一些应用来说，多视镜更好、更容易，但不是这个。
Ghost是自主的，由机载AI Core提供动力，每秒可以执行32万亿次操作。这比大多数这种大小的无人机多数百倍，我们用它来支持复杂的计算
Ghost是紧凑的，不仅是因为提供的能力，而且是绝对的。为了进行另一个比较，Ghost可以打包成一个比DJI Inspire手提箱还小的袋子！这在你想从一辆卡车上运输、携带和部署数十个Ghost时很重要，或者只是把一个单位随身携带到现场。
Ghost对它观察到的目标几乎是看不见的，它的正面横截面比我多年来拥有的一些手机都要小。请继续关注真正的隐身。
无论发生什么，幽灵都能看到你，使用稳定的可见光成像、热成像和其他专门的传感器，所有这些都被我们的AI核心融合到一张照片中。
Ghost非常坚固，由加工精良的金属和碳纤维复合材料混合而成，带有直驱电机和最小的磨损部件。我们并不是试图创造飞行耐力纪录-我们的首要任务是制造能够在疏忽、沙漠高温、北极寒冷、大风、强烈冲击、恶劣的战斗环境和盐水浸没中幸存下来的东西。我们的大多数竞争对手都会被这些东西中的任何一种击败，但我们仍然设法飞行得更长。
Ghost是模块化的，永远不会过时，飞机顶部和底部的有效载荷导轨为Anduril和第三方的新功能提供了电力和数据通道，从电子战到扬声器，再到卫星通信到激光指示器。与其他只有一个附件连接点(或没有)的无人机不同，我们在顶部容纳五个标准有效载荷模块，在底部容纳尽可能多的空间和重量。
幽灵不会使用疯狂的电源(目前还没有)。我们的电池组使用的是特斯拉和三星等公司使用的同一类型的21700电池组-不是能量密度最高的电池，但我们可以(也已经)使用模块化轨道安装的电源与更具异国情调的电池化学物质、汽油和氢燃料一起改变我们的耐力。“我们没有通过科博会项目技术获得很大的耐力，我们使用了最稳定、负担得起、在后勤上最可持续的技术，这要困难得多。未来还会有更多的其他电源。
Ghost从一开始就是为集群和团队而设计的，允许一个操作员在遥远的距离控制大量的网状网络飞机，有时是从世界的另一端。“这比作为一个操作员在本地控制一架无人机要困难得多，但战术回报是显而易见的。”这是可能的，因为我们之前在Lattice上的工作，它允许我们将来自数千个来源的数据融合到一个单一的人工智能驱动的协作共同操作图中。我们与Ghost所做的工作正在进入我们的另一个未完成的操作画面中。“Ghost”是一种人工智能驱动的协作操作画面，它允许我们将数千个来源的数据融合到一个单一的人工智能驱动的协作操作画面中。我们与Ghost所做的工作正在进入我们的另一个未完成的操作画面。
所有其他你想象中的幽灵在未来会做的事情，它都会做。
如果没有资格认证，很难称任何设备为“最好的”。但总会有具体的比较，比如某一特定功能优先于其他功能的地方，或者关于语义、定义和不成熟技术的研究实验室演示的争论。但在这种情况下，我非常放心地说，“幽灵4号”是目前最好的无人机。我迫不及待地想与美国战士及其全球盟友一起将最新版本送上天空-订单现在就开始，发货从2021年第一季度开始。我非常感谢帮助我们从简陋的原型到生产的合作伙伴，特别是未来突击队。
呵呵，今年CES不来真的很郁闷，我想在无人机展区中间带着一个Ghost 4的大展位现身，上面挂满了旗帜，还有一个大横幅，上面写着“最好的无人机是美国的无人机”，也许是Ghost 5呢？
note_en:
I’ve been going to the Consumer Electronics Show (CES) every January ever since I wasn’t allowed to, back when I was 16 years old with a specious ModRetro business card and little tech industry experience beyond my very first prototype of what would eventually become the Oculus Rift. While Anduril doesn’t make consumer electronics, I still attend every year to scout out new technology and meet with friends, partners, and suppliers who brave the Vegas crowds in search of tech esoterica.
 Twelve years of watching drone technology at CES emerge and then boom has made one thing abundantly clear: China Won.  American companies have built a lot of cool science fair projects that get lots of media attention, but minimal real-world deployment – from light shows to food delivery.  We also design a lot of interesting low-volume consumer drones, but most of them lean heavily on Chinese R&D and then go on to have their product manufactured in China.  Yes, I know, I am fully aware I did the same thing years ago with Oculus.
 This situation would piss me off a lot less if China was only winning the sales game.  As a PC gamer, I intimately understand that the cheaper option (consoles) sell very well given their ease of use and affordability.  In the case of drones, though, China leads the global drone industry across the entire spectrum, not only in quantity, but in quality!  DJI dominates the high end by building incredible imaging, networking, power, and aero systems while hundreds of Shenzhen-located companies nip at their heels by cranking out cheaper mid-range and low-end drones, along with most of the offerings designed by Western companies.  DJI might get temporarily beaten here and there by some upstart focusing on a specific feature or specification, but when it comes to the overall package, they rarely even get close.  When it comes to consumers, that overall package is what matters.
 Sadly, this is also true for militaries around the world, including the United States and our allies.  Given the amazing capabilities offered by the latest consumer drone technology, many units with any sort of purchasing authority discarded US-made drones that cost literally hundreds of thousands of dollars in favor of Chinese drones that cost just a few hundred dollars, driven by desire for compact size, great image quality, ease of use (especially during takeoff and landing) and the ability to hover in place rather than flying orbits (circles) around ever-changing views of their targets.  The 2019-2020 National Defense Authorization Act banned most government entities from purchasing or using Chinese drones, which inspired quite a few well-meaning but half-hearted attempts by US drone startups to adapt hobbyist and consumer drone designs for military service.  Designs that were optimized around the needs of consumers and hobbyists are rarely the right answer for brave users who spend a little more and demand a heck of a lot more, from security to durability to endurance and beyond.  In other words, the men and women who serve on the front lines.
 This is why we built  Ghost.  It is American-designed, American-made, and built from the ground up for the needs of warfighters, the most demanding users in the world.  We threw out the template everyone has been using for a decade and started from first principles, using world-class design to make something extraordinary.  We didn’t invent any new chemistry, metallurgy, or physics, we just used the same materials available to everyone in a very new way.  Warfighters have already been using previous versions of Ghost for a while now, but we are finally able to talk more publicly about our work in this space as we launch Ghost 4.
 I can’t possibly cover all of the elements of Ghost that make it the best drone there is (that a person can carry, anyway – Predators don’t count) because there are just too many things to cover.  The best way to try is probably comparisons with a few elements of other drones people are familiar with:
 Ghost is a single-rotor aircraft.  The mechanicals of the rotor system are harder to design and manufacture than a DJI-style quadcopter, and flight controls are harder to program, but the result is much lower aerodynamic disk loading that results in longer endurance (over 100 minutes with real mission payloads), near-silent acoustic signature, high max payload capacity (dozens of pounds), and high speed.  Compare this with a DJI Inspire 2, a high-end offering that costs $3000 that can lumber around with a couple pounds for 24 to 27 minutes, props screaming.  If we wanted to create the best marketing numbers, we could fly a drone with nothing on it for even longer, but that isn’t my style.  Multicopters are better and easier for some applications, but not this.
 Ghost is autonomous, powered by an onboard AI Core that can perform 32 trillion operations per second.  That is hundreds of times more than most drones this size, and we use it to power complex computer vision and sensor fusion algorithms that make Ghost into something less like a manually operated RC plane or blob-following quadcopter and more like a real pilot, capable of making decisions and acting on them with minimal human guidance.  The operator can rely on Ghost to reliably perform all kinds of tasks for them, even when communications are jammed or turned off to avoid detection.  It is hard to overstate how much software work is involved in this capability – the future of warfare will be superficially hardware-driven with a buttload of software smarts under the hood making it all actually work.
 Ghost is compact, not just for the capabilities provided, but in absolute terms.  To make another comparison, Ghost packs up into a bag that is even smaller than the DJI Inspire carry case!  This is important when you want to ship, carry, and deploy dozens of Ghosts from a single truck, or just carry a single unit with you into the field.
 Ghost is practically invisible to the targets it observes, with a frontal cross-section smaller than some of the phones I have owned over the years.  Stay tuned for true invisibility.
 Ghost sees you no matter what, using stabilized visible spectrum imaging, thermal imaging, and other specialized sensors all fused into a single picture by our AI Core.
 Ghost is incredibly rugged, a mix of well-machined metals and carbon fiber composites with a direct-drive motor and minimal wear components.  We weren’t trying to set flight endurance records – our top priority was to make something that could survive neglect, desert heat, arctic cold, high winds, heavy shock, abusive combat environments, and submersion in salt water.  Most of our competition will get taken out by any one of those things, and we still manage to fly far longer.
 Ghost is modular and never obsolete, with payload rails on the top and bottom of the aircraft that provide access to power and data for new capabilities from both Anduril and third-parties, from electronic warfare to loudspeakers to satcom to laser designators.  Unlike other drones that have a single accessory attachment point (or none), we accommodate five standard payload modules on the top and as many as space and weight allow on the bottom.
 Ghost doesn’t use a crazy power source (yet). Our battery packs are utilizing the same type of 21700 battery cells used by companies like Tesla and Samsung – not the most energy-dense cells out there, but we can (and have) changed our endurance using modular-rail mounted power supplies with more exotic battery chemistries, gasoline, and hydrogen fuel.  We didn’t get great endurance with science fair project technology, we did it with the most stable, affordable, and logistically-sustainable tech out there, which was much harder.  More to come on other power sources in the future.
 Ghost is designed from the start for swarming and teaming, allowing a single operator to control large numbers of mesh-networked airframes across vast distances, sometimes from the other side of the world.  This is so much harder than locally controlling a single drone as a single operator, but the tactical rewards are obvious.  This is only possible because of our previous work on Lattice, which allows us to fuse data from thousands of sources into a single AI-driven collaborative common operating picture.  The work we have done with Ghost is going into our other unannounced drone projects as well.
 All those other things you imagine Ghost is going to do in the future, it is going to do.
 It is hard to call any device “the best” without qualification.  There will always be specific comparisons where a particular feature was prioritized at the expense of others, or arguments about semantics, definitions, and research lab demonstrations of immature technologies.  In this case, though, I feel very comfortable saying that  Ghost 4 is the best drone there is.  I can’t wait to get the latest version into the skies with US warfighters and their allies across the globe – orders start right now, shipments start in Q1 2021. I owe a huge thank you to the partners who have helped us go from janky prototype to production, especially the Future Commando Force.
 Oh, and I am really bummed that CES is not happening this year.  I wanted to show up with a big booth for Ghost 4 in the middle of the drone section, with plenty of flags and a big banner proclaiming “THE BEST DRONES ARE AMERICAN DRONES”.  Maybe Ghost 5?
title: 中国制造了最好的无人机，现在安杜里尔制造了
title_en: China made the best drones, now Anduril does

note:
近30年来，德国首次在全国范围内进行紧急预警。但并不是每件事都按计划进行。
上午11点。夏普(格林威治时间0900)德国在全国范围内对其民用警报系统进行了测试-从警报器到智能手机上的推送通知都在测试中。这项测试原定运行时间正好是20分钟。
根据保护和灾害援助办公室(BBK)的说法，国家紧急警报日旨在测试德国的警报系统，并让公众为国家紧急状态下的应对做好准备。
一方面，这是关于对警报系统进行技术测试。另一个是我们希望通过警告日来提高民众的敏感度。BBK负责人克里斯托夫·昂格尔(Christoph Unger)表示：我们想让他们了解警报器等警告信号的含义。
虽然德国许多地区都响起了警报声，但在紧急考试日，其他地区却出现了坎坷的铺开-特别是随着更现代的技术的普及。
这是第一次在全国范围内发送紧急推送通知-但许多用户报告说，要么根本没有收到通知，要么在延迟一段时间后收到了通知。
只有在延迟之后才能收到全国性的MOWAS[模块化警告系统]。BBK在推特上写道，原因是计划外同时通过MoWAS触发了大量警告信息。
该系统使用卫星系统向公共广播公司、新闻机构、关键基础设施公司以及安装了所谓的警告应用程序的智能手机用户发送警告信息。
然而，这一全国性的推广似乎使系统不堪重负-引发了社交媒体用户的一波笑话、批评和困惑。
电视和无线电广播本应中断，但据报道，并非所有波长都是这样。
火车站的数字标志将在站台上显示紧急信息，数字广告牌将显示紧急测试信息。
从上午11点开始，空袭警报连续响起一分钟，测试音调，然后改变音调。
目前全国仍在使用的警报器从1993年的8万个减少到现在的15000个。德国冷战时期的大部分警报器系统已经年久失修，许多警报器都被拆除了。
德国人口最多的北莱茵-威斯特伐利亚州的内政部发布了指导方针，告知公众不同的警报声以及它们发出的警报类型。
定期改变音调且没有中断的警报器表示警告。警报器不改变音调，但有两次有规律的中断，这是消防部门的警报。最后，持续一分钟的单音标志着紧急情况的结束。
这些测试是一种让公众做好准备，以便在国家紧急状态下做什么以及去哪里看的一种方式。
此类紧急情况可能包括洪水、地震、火灾和其他自然灾害，以及停电、放射性排放和核警告。
内政部议会国务秘书斯蒂芬·梅耶尔(Stephan Mayer)解释说，许多官员认为，柏林墙倒塌后，不再需要预警系统。
梅耶尔告诉发行量很大的“图片报”(Bild)，统一后，我们认为不再需要警笛了。
虽然核弹袭击的威胁没有那么严重，但官员们忘记了自然灾害和其他事件可能会经常发生。
未来，政府计划将测试作为一年一度的活动，将9月10日指定为紧急警报测试日。
note_en:
For the first time in almost 30 years, Germany carried out a nationwide emergency warning day. But not everything went as planned.
    At 11 a.m. sharp (0900 GMT) Germany carried out a nationwide test of its civil alarm systems — with everything from sirens to push notifications on smartphones being tested. The test was slated to run for exactly 20 minutes.
      According to the Office for Protection and Disaster Aid (BBK), the national emergency warning day is intended to test out Germany's warning systems and prepare the public for what to do in the event of a national emergency.
  "On the one hand, this is about conducting a technical test of the warning systems. The other is that we want to sensitize the population with the warning day. We want to give them an understanding of what such warning signals, such as the sirens, mean," said Christoph Unger, the head of the BBK.
    While sirens wailed across many parts of Germany, the emergency test day saw a bumpy roll out in other areas — particularly with more modern technology.
  It was the first time that nationwide emergency push notifications were due to be sent out — but many users reported either not receiving a notification at all or getting one after a delay.
  "The nationwide MoWaS [Modular warning system] could only be received after a delay. The reason for this was an unscheduled simultaneous triggering of a large number of warning messages via MoWaS," the BBK wrote on Twitter.
  The system uses a satellite system to send out warning messages to public broadcasters, news agencies, critical infrastructure companies — and smartphone users with so-called "warning apps."
    The nationwide push, however, appeared to overload the system — prompting a wave of jokes, criticism, and confusion from social media users.
  TV and radio broadcasts were supposed to be interrupted, but that was reportedly not the case on all wavelengths.
  Digital signs at train stations were to show emergency messages on the platform and digital advertising boards were to show emergency test messages.
    Air raid sirens sounded for one straight minute starting at 11 a.m., testing their tones full blast before varying the pitch of the tone.
  There are around 15,000 sirens still in use across the country, down from 80,000 in 1993. Much of Germany's Cold War siren system has fallen into disrepair, with numerous sirens dismantled.
  The Interior Ministry of Germany's most populous state, North Rhine-Westphalia, put out guidelines to inform the public about the different siren tones and what type of an alert they signal.
  A siren that regularly changes its pitch with no breaks indicates a warning. A siren that doesn't change its pitch but has two regular breaks is an alert for the fire department. And finally, a singular tone that lasts for a minute signals the end of an emergency.
      The tests are a way to prep the public for what to do and where to look in the event of a national emergency.
  Such emergencies could include floods, earthquakes, fires and other natural disasters, as well as electricity outages, radioactive emissions and nuclear warnings.
    Stephan Mayer, the parliamentary state secretary for the Interior Ministry, explained that many officials thought that the warning systems weren't needed any longer after the fall of the Berlin Wall.
  "After reunification, we believed there was no need for sirens anymore," Mayer told the mass-circulation  Bild newspaper.
  Although the threat of nuclear bomb attacks was less acute, officials forgot to consider that natural disasters and other incidents can regularly occur.
  In the future, the government plans on making the tests an annual event, designating September 10 as emergency alarm test day.
title: 德国几十年来首次在全国范围内进行紧急预警
title_en: Germany conducts first nationwide emergency warning day in decades

note:
拥有TikTok的中国公司字节跳动(ByteDance)正面临9月15日的最后期限，要么出售其美国业务，要么让特朗普政府关闭这些业务。周四晚上，特朗普总统告诉记者，最后期限不会推迟。
特朗普在登上飞往密歇根的航班前表示：出于安全原因，我们要么关闭TikTok在美国的业务，要么将其出售。我不会延长最后期限。不是的。今天是9月15日。TikTok的截止日期不会延长。
有报道称，微软和甲骨文都对ByteDance的美国业务提出了收购要约。问题是，这些公司可能无法安抚美国和中国政府相互冲突的要求。
8月初，特朗普宣布TikTok是国家安全威胁，因为中国政府有可能收集美国用户的数据，并在美国开展造谣运动。特朗普声称，新的国际紧急经济权力法案赋予总统权力，如果TikTok这样的应用威胁到国家安全，就可以关闭它。
8月14日的一项后续命令追溯阻止了字节跳动2017年对Musical.ly的收购，该平台后来演变为TikTok。总统有权阻止引发国家安全担忧的国际合并，一些专家认为这是关闭该服务的更强有力的法律依据。
ByteDance同时接受了微软和甲骨文的报价，据报道，当中国政府介入时，ByteDance接近选择其中之一。8月底，北京宣布了新的出口管制规定，限制未经政府批准的人工智能软件出口。
TikTok拥有复杂的视频推荐算法，很可能会与这些规则相冲突。因此，新规则意味着，任何交易都必须得到中国政府的批准--中国政府不希望美国夺取中国最受欢迎的全球技术平台之一的控制权。
有一种危险是，美国和中国政府相互冲突的要求将不会给TikTok留下与微软、甲骨文或其他任何公司达成交易的空间。在这种情况下，ByteDance可能会被迫关闭其美国业务。
但目前还不清楚禁令将于何时真正生效。特朗普告诉记者，字节跳动需要在9月15日之前达成协议。但特朗普的书面行政命令给出了不同的最后期限：第一个行政命令在9月20日左右，第二个行政命令在11月中旬。这些最后期限也可能会被法院推迟，因为他们考虑到字节跳动的论点，即这些命令本身是非法的。
嗯。我所看到的只是青少年和成年人每天都在这个肤浅的、物质主义的视频平台上浪费几个小时。最好是永久禁止。
好极了！我们这些有素质的人来Ars是为了表达我们对那些把时间浪费在互联网上的人的蔑视。
note_en:
ByteDance, the Chinese company that owns TikTok, is facing a looming September 15 deadline to sell its US operations or have them shut down by the Trump administration. On Thursday evening, President Trump  told reporters that the deadline wouldn't be pushed back.
 "We'll either close up TikTok in this country for security reasons, or it'll be sold," Trump said just before boarding a flight to Michigan. "I'm not extending deadlines. No. It's September 15. There will be no extension of the TikTok deadline."
 Reports indicate that both Microsoft and Oracle have made offers for ByteDance's US operations. The problem is that the companies may not be able to appease the conflicting demands of the US and Chinese governments.
  In early August, Trump  declared TikTok a national security threat due to the potential for the Chinese government to collect data on American users and to conduct disinformation campaigns in the United States. Trump claimed that the International Emergency Economic Powers Act gives the president the authority to shut down an app like TikTok if it threatens national security.
 A  follow-up order on August 14 retroactively blocked ByteDance's  2017 acquisition of Musical.ly, the platform that subsequently evolved into TikTok. The president has the power to block international mergers that raise national security concerns, and some experts see this as a stronger legal basis for shutting down the service.
 ByteDance fielded offers from both  Microsoft and  Oracle and was reportedly close to choosing one of them when the Chinese government intervened. At the end of August, Beijing  announced new export control rules that limited exports of artificial intelligence software without government approval.
 TikTok has sophisticated video recommendation algorithms that would likely run afoul of those rules. So the new rules mean that any sale would have to be approved by the Chinese government—which would prefer not to have the US seize control of one of China's most popular global technology platforms.
 There's a danger that the conflicting demands of the US and Chinese governments will leave no room for TikTok to do a deal with Microsoft, Oracle, or anyone else. In that case, ByteDance might be forced to shut down its US operations.
 But it's not clear when a ban would actually take effect. Trump has told reporters that ByteDance needs to do a deal by September 15. But Trump's written executive orders gave different deadlines: around September 20 for the first executive order and mid-November for the second one. These deadlines could also be pushed back by the courts as they consider  ByteDance's arguments that the orders themselves are illegal.
  Meh. All I see is teenagers and adults alike wasting hours a day in this shallow, materialistic video platform. Better to perma ban it.
 Right on! We quality folk come to Ars to express our disdain for those who fritter away their time on the internet.
title: 特朗普誓言不会将TikTok的最后期限延长到9月15日之后
title_en: Trump vows not to extend TikTok deadline beyond September 15
